title: podman system reset runroot to /tmp/container-user-<id>
body:
### Issue Description

Using podman 4.9 in rootless mode, if a user perform podman system reset, the value of runroot is set to /tmp/container-user-<id> instead of /run/user/<id>. On some distro RHEL by default tmp isnt a tmpfs which leads to an error.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
As a regular podman user perform
1. podman info # watch for runroot 
2. podman system reset
3. podman info  # watch for runroot


### Describe the results you received

Upon container restart error message about /tmp/container-user-<id> is not a tmpfs and some distribution can sometime even not clean those folders (tmp.conf) malingre netavark unable to résolve internal container.

### Describe the results you expected

Podman system reset saoule go back to what is by default /run/user

### podman info output

```yaml
Podman 4.9
Rocky linux 8.10
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Are you running with a proper systemd user session and XDG_RUNTIME_DIR defined and set to /run/user/...? Because if not podman has to fall back to /tmp to get a writeable temporary directory.
Also not upstream only support the lastest version, 4.9 is quite old so please test with 5.5.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).
i think the fallback behavior is intentional and documented and It's a safety mechanism, not a bug
Ok I validated now the issue happened because XDG_RUNTIME_DIR wasn't set properly.
